design_philosophy.core_loop: Side-scrolling beat 'em up combat through city levels; defeat waves of criminals and supervillain minions using Spider-Man and Venom's melee attacks; summon allied Marvel heroes via collectibles for tactical advantage
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Progression through densely-packed enemy gauntlets with manageable resources (health, hero summons); balancing when to spend summoned heroes vs. reserve them for boss encounters
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as both Spider-Man and Venom, two iconic rival characters forced into temporary partnership; command an expanding roster of Marvel heroes (Captain America, Iron Fist, Black Cat, Deathlok, Morbius, Firestar) as backup fighters; thematically experience the comic story arc of Maximum Carnage
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Committed adaptation of specific comic book story arc with semi-animated cutscenes directly sourced from comic panels; dual-character gameplay with asymmetric hero support (summoned heroes favor certain characters); deliberately long and repetitive experience suggesting intentional difficulty scaling through attrition rather than mechanical complexity

difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based progression structure implied; no detailed checkpoint documentation available
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited difficulty settings documented; player choice in hero summoning strategy provides some tactical agency (deciding which summoned allies to deploy at which moments)

progression.upgrade_system: Summoned hero assistance system; collectible items hidden in set locations unlock allied Marvel characters with varying effectiveness based on which protagonist (Spider-Man vs. Venom) activated them
progression.unlock_mechanism: Finding power-up items in predetermined level locations to unlock temporary summoned hero assistance; heroes aid combat in different ways depending on which character summoned them
replayability_hooks: Asymmetric hero summon effectiveness encourages replaying with different characters to test summoning strategies
Two playable protagonists (Spider-Man and Venom) with presumably different playstyles
Hidden collectible item locations reward exploration and knowledge accumulation across playthroughs

world_building_techniques: Semi-animated comic-book style cutscenes using artwork directly sourced from the Maximum Carnage comic story arc
Direct adaptation of Marvel comic narrative (Eddie Brock/Venom proving innocence, bonding to Cletus Kasady, Carnage formation, villain recruitment chain)
Environmental storytelling through level progression in New York City (sewers, city streets)
Comic-based visual presentation of boss encounters and character confrontations

boss_design.philosophy: Iconic supervillain encounters based on comic story arc; bosses represent Carnage's recruited team (Shriek, Doppelganger, Demogoblin, Carrion) and culminate in final Carnage battle; boss encounters integrated into narrative progression
notable_bosses: Carnage (final boss; defeats Spider-Man and Venom initially, later defeated after hero alliance is formed)
Demogoblin (Hobgoblin empowered after rejection by Carnage)

faction_indicators: Spider-Man & Venom Alliance: forced partnership between rivals to combat greater threat (Carnage)
Carnage's Criminal Empire: recruitment-based villain team in NYC sewers showing organizational structure
Marvel Hero Coalition: resistance formed from Spider-Man's previous allies (Captain America, Black Cat, Iron Fist, Cloak & Dagger, Deathlok, Morbius, Firestar)
danger_signals: Carnage's escape into city sewers and establishment of criminal headquarters represents territorial escalation
Serial recruitment of increasingly powerful villains (Shriek, then Demogoblin, then Doppelganger, then Carrion) suggests coordinated threat escalation
Initial near-fatal encounter between Spider-Man/Venom and Carnage team signals overwhelming enemy capability
environmental_storytelling: New York City locations serve as stages of Carnage's territory expansion
Sewer levels represent villain headquarters and organizational center
Multiple district/environment progression implies city-wide threat distribution

sources_used: Wikipedia: Spider-Man and Venom: Maximum Carnage (gameplay, plot, development, reception sections)
The Cutting Room Floor: Maximum Carnage (SNES) (developer info, genre classification, critical reception context)
